name: MAYA: The Birth of a Superhero

reviews.review_description:
As a fellow immersive filmmaker, this experience touches me deeply. It tells the important and collective story of so many women and girls, daily experience of them but so often silenced and shamed. 

Everything was beautifully curated together, love the art style, hand tracking interaction and music, some stunning perspectives shift with experience design as well!

Well done!

For anyone wanting to buy it, it's not a game purely for fun, please do have the expectation for an immersive experience.

reviews.review_title: powerful story but point lost on many
reviews.review_description:
This is really an interactive movie and not a game. It's best to leave it until you've got enough time to relax and be immersed into the story and be receptive to the story of a young woman starting puberty.

As a father of two daughters, I watched this and it gave me insight into how starting periods affected them. I'll admit it made me uncomfortable but I think that's the point, I certainly understand better. 

There are many low rated reviews and it's clear the entire point of this app was lost to them. It's disappointing that those people didn't even get a glimmer of insight from it. Perhaps the description of the app could be better so as to set the viewer's expectation into a more receptive mindset?

The concept and execution of this movie is excellent, it is stylish and well done.

While it’s true that very primitive areas believe in “filth”, not true for any civilized nation.  Not for anyone that can afford a VR headset anyway.  Described this story to my wife and she couldn’t believe the utter nonsense.

Was led to believe this game was about super heroes. Instead I was dragged through a whole bunch of propaganda about how having a period makes you strong. 

Monster design sucked. Mom and dads sucked. 
Played this game for like a minute before realizing that if I was a parent I would not in a million years have shown this to my daughter. The visuals are wrong. Not disturbing...wrong. 

I might actually just report this game. Its this bad.

It was more of a story, I had no idea what it was about but the art looked cool and it was free. I liked the story, and loved the art! My first game being a non gamer on my quest 3, and I liked it. I may be able to get in more games like this that are more like a story. I usually only play dance type games, this was a good starter game for me.

Humans are weird creatures who have been out of touch with the natural order of the universe for centuries. Periods are completely natural. I hope this experience empowers girls going through their firsts to feel comfortable about their periods, as well as educating the ignorant men about a natural occurrence that women have to go through.
It was boring but disgusting enough that I stuck around out of curiosity… But when it crossed into disturbing I uninstalled.
I did not think this was about periods I was looking to play a game and the title tricked me if your are Man/boy this is not for you
A game about the menstrual cycle wasn't for me obviously, so a quick uninstall.
I don’t like the game because it’s really weird and I don’t want to know

While the unusual art style and abstract narrative may not appeal to everyone, this VR film tells an inspiring story about period stigma, addressing many cultural taboos surrounding this “controversial” topic.

I suggest being cautious of negative reviews, as the primary criticisms often label the game as “too feminist,” “not for men,” or “yucky,” ironically reinforcing the points made by the game. Although the game discusses periods and includes mild fantasy spookiness, there is nothing excessively inappropriate.

I recommend this VR film to those interested in learning about this frequently overlooked topic from a perspective that prioritises female empowerment.
The creators pulled from a lot of different artistic influences to make a really interesting experience and statement. Highly recommend to see what is being done in the VR space.
Uh… do not play if male, we are not the target audience. This was a quick uninstall

reviews.review_title: Listen to the one star reviews
reviews.review_description:
This experience although short, is an amazing immersive experience which uses that medium to create discussion around views of menstruation in different cultures. It should be self evident that this experience achieves this discussion by the numerous one star reviews claiming this piece to be 'disgusting' or 'scarring'. The power of immersion is that it physically allows you to see through someone else's eyes, and in this experience you feel it. You feel the shame and disgust surrounding menstruation that is perpetuated by our society. You feel the fear of being bullied and isolated for being on your period. And you feel that things need to change. 

Discussion around menstruation for those who don't have to experience it can be scary or gross, but it is our job as a society to support the people that go through it, not shame them because we don't like it.

My only negative comment is that the story revolves around her becoming a superhero from her period. This is a good metaphor for the bravery of confronting others about their opinions around menstruation, however this does tie in well with the idea that menstruation is dark magic and should be shamed, which doesn't really support the key theme.

Overall a really great experience that shows the potential VR has for storytelling
This game should be flagged for adult topics, game talking about puberty shouldn’t be allowed on here

reviews.review_title: Wasn’t what I expected at all 🥴
reviews.review_description: If you are a parent of a prepubescent or pubescent female teenage, or one yourself then maybe this is for you. After 5 minutes I uninstalled it. Goes into details of the stigma of the female period. Wasn’t my thing or what I thought the app might be. 🫣🤢🤮

I’m a middle aged father with a daughter. Due to this team’s extreme artistic ability, and the bravery to tackle such a taboo subject, I rate it 4 stars even though I can’t directly relate.

If I felt the urge to chuckle at points, that was my own need to do some growing up when I think about it. 

Beautiful imagery, difficult subject. Worth trying.
The design, the storyline such an amazing experience game gave me chills, wish it was a little longer but overall amazing for young girls out there.

reviews.review_title: I finally know what its like to have a .
reviews.review_description: This was very funny! I ☠️ at the part where the huge purgina (v word is profane apparently) swallows you whole. Flicking menstrual blood at those high school kids in the cafeteria was awesome as well. 10/10 highly recommend! 😂
It is a well made VR experience with fantasy ideas. It’s about 20-30 mins content. But totally worth it to play. Some scenes even are better than the paid app. Good job and nice concept.
I can’t get past the graphics to even dislike the story.  It looks like it was drawn by a child and gave me a headache very quickly
It had a deceptive title it wasn’t a fan. I can’t relate and overall gross. I thought it was a game.
The title of the so called game is deceptive, it's subject is gross, my wife couldn't even get past the tampon boat floating in period blood. Don't scar yourself for life and avoid this.
It's free. It's one of the best made VR cinematic experiences. It has a strong message. Hand tracking was used to immerse you more in the narrative. Artistic visuals. 30 minutes long.

reviews.review_title: a story with a message, impressively!
reviews.review_description: the subject discussed here may be a bit over the top in the Western world. Given the freedom we live in and where it is possible to talk freely about all topics and matters that concern you, you forget that this is certainly not the case everywhere. We quickly dismiss a topic that we are comfortable talking about by venting our own projection on this topic. If you delve into it a little further, you will know that this is a sensitive topic in many places in the world. This subject is taboo in many places and it is precisely by portraying this subject in such a cinematic way that you activate the conversation about it. Much admiration for the makers. It is not a game, but a message that may make it possible to break the taboo surrounding periods. Yes, the images are sometimes a bit confrontational, but the beautiful cinematic representation makes them a true immersion. A story with a message. And you don't have to watch it. But if you do, delve deeper into the subject and experience that it is a great asset to be able to talk about these types of topics and that this is not possible in many places in the world.
Waste of time
I’m not sure what this is intended for but it feels like feminist propaganda.
The title can easily trick you and I find this game kinda boring and weird.
Stories matter. Will update when able.

reviews.review_title: Visuals are very nice story a fail
reviews.review_description:
However. It stretches a bit at some points i wished it where faster.

Talks about a important topic but I don't like the way they try to adress the topic. I would call it a fail.

So check it out for the good visuals but dont expect much from the story its a disappointment. all the magic stuff almost contradicting itself when critizising that magic is used to explain something normal amd then use mavic as an alternative to explain things Strange concept and it doesnt work.

Poulomi Basu challenges us to live real life in this factual fantasy that strips the camouflage and euphemisms from the experience of menstruation.  The story of Maya is inspiring, invigorating and for many of us educational.  The tale unfolds in a gripping narrative that is both unworldly and very much of this world.  Grab your headset and fasten your seatbelts - it's a ride!

This is some next level feminist garbage.  The movie is rooted in mysticism and witchcraft... perfect... just what young impressionable girls DONT need more of.  Themed in Hindu culture, this movie has some nice artwork but overall the theme about a girl having her period and becoming some kind of superhero with superpowers is unrealistic and frankly unhelpful to and young person watching this.
I have been scard for life got 5 min in and had to turn it off😭😭😭👎👎👎
Powerful experience
First of all it’s talking about Periods but I like that it’s magic and the rest was much of a story like I was a third and first person
I like the game but it had stuff like t@mp0ns and it talked about per!0ds but other than that it was great

I might be wrong but I believe that this content is designed for young girls. In this case, the way the author decided to talk about the topic is quite disturbing. It's easy to understand that the goal was to prepare and provide an example of courage but on the other hand, what it accomplishes is completely different teaching fear and anger. I'm so glad I checked the content before suggesting it to anyone else
